mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, triggering risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month walk-forward test, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains above -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, capacity utilization approaches limits, prompting expectations of supply bottlenecks and margin compression that generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Hypothesis is rejected if INDPRO growth above 2% annualized correlates with positive 20-day returns in > 65% of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 18-month window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling test, or threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index combined with stagnant real GDP growth indicates stagflationary pressure, which erodes corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive CPIAUCSL growth alongside flat GDPC1 yields positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In early recovery phases, accelerating industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and operational leverage, which expands corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Hypothesis fails if INDPRO acceleration correlates with negative 5-day returns in > 55% of early-cycle observations, or regime classification yields no significant alpha over baseline.
